title: Mathematics + Financial Literacy: Class 9 — Statistics and Probability
subject: Mathematics

questions.text: Stock prices of a company for 5 days: ₹100, ₹105, ₹98, ₹112, ₹95. What is the mean stock price? What does this tell an investor?

options.text: ₹100, the stock is at its original price
₹102, the average daily price — useful for buy/sell decision
₹98, the minimum price
₹105, the maximum price

questions.explanation: Mean = (100+105+98+112+95)/5 = 510/5 = ₹102. Investors use the 50-day and 200-day moving average (mean of last 50/200 days prices) to decide buy/sell signals. When current price > 200-day mean, it's often considered a 'buy' signal.
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.text: Monthly salaries in a company: ₹8,000, ₹9,000, ₹10,000, ₹11,000, ₹2,00,000 (CEO). Should HR report mean or median as 'typical salary'? Why?

options.text: Mean (₹47,600), because it uses all data points
Median (₹10,000), because it's not skewed by the CEO's extreme salary
Mode, because it's the most common salary
Mean always represents a typical employee

questions.explanation: Mean = (8000+9000+10000+11000+200000)/5 = ₹47,600 — misleading! Median = ₹10,000 — the middle value, not affected by outliers. This is why economists report 'median household income' not 'mean' — India's median income is far below mean due to billionaires skewing the average.
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.text: A bag has 5 blue chips (profit) and 3 red chips (loss) in a stock trading game. If you pick one randomly, what is P(profit)? How do insurance companies use this concept?

options.text: 5/8; insurers calculate probability of claims to set premiums
5/3; insurers use ratios for risk
3/8; loss probability is what matters for insurance
1/2; it's always 50-50 in business

questions.explanation: P(profit) = 5/8 = 0.625. Insurance companies calculate P(claim) similarly. If P(car accident) = 0.05 and average repair = ₹50,000, expected payout = 0.05 × 50,000 = ₹2,500/year. Premium must exceed this. Your probability chapter is actuarial science!
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.text: A small business has: Fixed Costs = ₹20,000/month, Revenue per unit = ₹50, Variable Cost per unit = ₹30. Write the break-even equation and find the break-even quantity.
questions.explanation: Break-even: Total Revenue = Total Costs. 50x = 30x + 20000. 20x = 20000. x = 1000 units. This is a linear equation! Every startup founder uses this formula. If they sell < 1000 units, they lose money; > 1000 units, they profit.
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.text: The number of students who passed an exam over 5 years: 60, 72, 84, 96, 108. Is this an AP? If yes, find the number who will pass in year 8.

options.text: Yes AP with d=12; Year 8 = 144 students
Yes AP with d=12; Year 8 = 132 students
No, not an AP
Yes AP with d=12; Year 8 = 156 students

questions.explanation: Differences: 72-60=12, 84-72=12, 96-84=12 → AP with a=60, d=12. For year 8: a₈ = 60 + (8-1)×12 = 60+84 = 144. Companies use AP models for linear growth projections in annual reports!
questions.subject: Mathematics
